idea: Blue-green service rollout with ingress, autoscaling, and network policy
why_this_project_matters: This project models a safer release process for shared clusters by separating old and new versions and explicitly controlling traffic flow.
architecture: Two Deployments represent blue and green versions of the same service. A shared Service forwards traffic to the currently active color, while Ingress exposes the route externally. HPA keeps both versions scalable and NetworkPolicy restricts pod-to-pod communication to expected paths.
steps: Apply the namespace, services, and both deployment manifests.
Validate that the ingress reaches the blue version before switching traffic selectors.
Patch the Service selector to point at the green version during rollout testing.
Monitor autoscaling and pod health during the cutover window.
Roll back by restoring the Service selector if the green version fails validation.
